title: Genoa FCC curve datasets

f5050.method: 47 CFR §73.333 Figure 1 — F(50,50) for the FM band (88–108 MHz)

axes.haat_m: Antenna height above average terrain, meters
axes.f_dBu: Field strength in dBu (dB above 1 µV/m)
axes.value: Distance in km to that field strength at 1 kW ERP

scaling.erp_kW: field strength shifts by 10·log10(ERP_kW); apply BEFORE distance lookup
scaling.out_of_range_haat: below 30 m or above 1200 m — caller MUST dispatch to itmlogic for ITM-grade answer

f5050.provenance: Polynomial fit to §73.333 Fig. 1 published curves; reference HAAT=300 m row matches the FCC tabulation. Other HAATs scaled per §73.333 Note 4. Replace with verbatim TVStudy curve export for filing-grade accuracy.

f5010.provenance: Derived from F(50,50) base curves with the §73.333 Fig. 1a published F(50,10) − F(50,50) field-strength offset (~+5 dB at typical service distances), expressed as a distance ratio. Replace with verbatim TVStudy curve export for filing-grade accuracy.

am_groundwave.method: 47 CFR §73.184 Figures M3 (groundwave conductivity attenuation curves)

axes.value: Sommerfeld attenuation factor A (unitless, multiplies the unattenuated 100·sqrt(P_kW) mV/m at 1 km reference)

am_groundwave.provenance: Norton 1941 Sommerfeld attenuation factor evaluated at the FCC §73.184 conductivity grid. Conductivity-dependent shape adjustments applied to match the published Fig. M3 curves at the standard distances. Replace with FCC tabulated A(d, f, σ) for filing-grade AM exhibits.

_notes: Each curve dataset is published as a separate JSON file with its own sha256. The exhibit reproducibility package cites the meta.json hash AND the per-dataset hashes, so any later edit to the curve data invalidates a prior exhibit's signature.
Tier-1 milestone: replace these polynomial-fit values with the FCC TVStudy curve files (study_files/curve_data) for filing-grade accuracy. The schema and lookup code do not change.
